title: EDA/SIP Revenue Up 12.7%; APAC Roars Back
gist: Worldwide EDA, semiconductor IP, and services revenue hit $5.748 billion in Q1 2026, up 12.7% year over year, driven by AI-server-chip design-tool demand and a 31% jump in China sales after export-restriction relaxation; Asia/Pacific and EMEA led regional growth while Japan declined.

insights.claim: Worldwide EDA, semiconductor IP, and services revenue reached $5.748 billion in Q1 2026, a 12.7% year-over-year increase, with the industry's four-quarter moving average run rate now above $22 billion versus roughly $10 billion in the past.
insights.speaker: Ed Sperling
insights.so_what: EDA/IP revenue growth running well above its own historical trend (run rate now ~2.2x its former $10B level) confirms design-tool spending is scaling with, not lagging, the broader AI silicon buildout — a durable secular tailwind for EDA vendors (Synopsys, Cadence, Siemens EDA, Silvaco).
insights.quote: The run rate is over $22 billion. In the past, the run rate was more like $10 billion. The four-quarter moving average is 10.3%, so this is above the moving average.

insights.claim: Computer-aided engineering (CAE) tools, the category most tied to AI server chip design, grew fastest among EDA segments at 15.5% to $2.018 billion in Q1 2026, identified as the key driver of overall EDA/IP revenue growth.
insights.speaker: Ed Sperling
insights.so_what: CAE's outperformance versus other EDA categories signals that AI-chip design activity specifically (not general semiconductor design) is the dominant growth driver — a segment-level data point for evaluating EDA vendors' AI-exposure mix.

insights.claim: Chinese EDA/IP sales rose 31% to $508 million in Q1 2026 (versus $388 million in Q1 2025) following a relaxation of export restrictions, marking a notable rebound in a previously constrained market.
insights.speaker: Ed Sperling
insights.so_what: The China EDA rebound is a direct, measurable market signal of export-policy easing; investors tracking US-China semiconductor tech-transfer policy can use this 31% growth figure as a leading indicator of further loosening or tightening in subsequent quarters.

insights.claim: Japan was the only major region where EDA/IP revenue did not grow in Q1 2026, declining 9.9% to $283.1 million, while Wally Rhines noted that excluding SIP, every other region grew, mostly in double digits.
insights.speaker: Ed Sperling
insights.so_what: Japan's EDA weakness stands out against a globally strong quarter, worth investigating whether it reflects local semiconductor design-spend softness or yen-driven reporting effects before drawing conclusions about the Japanese chip design sector.
insights.quote: Japan was the only region that didn’t grow. But if you exclude SIP, everything grew, and most of that was in the double digits.

insights.claim: Asia/Pacific EDA/IP revenue grew 17.7% to $2.264 billion and EMEA grew 17.6% to $766.4 million in Q1 2026, both outpacing the Americas' 10.2% growth to $2.434 billion, indicating the growth center of gravity is shifting toward APAC and EMEA even though the Americas remains the largest region by dollar revenue.
insights.speaker: Ed Sperling
insights.so_what: APAC's near-18% growth rate approaching the Americas' absolute revenue size suggests EDA vendors with strong APAC channel presence and China/Korea/Taiwan customer relationships are capturing disproportionate growth versus those weighted toward the US market.

insights.claim: South Korea and India EDA revenues were flat in Q1 2026; in Korea this occurred even as memory makers Samsung and SK hynix saw DRAM revenue increases (which don't typically flow through to design-tool spend), while India's flatness appears to reflect multinational companies not breaking out region-specific EDA spend for cross-border designs rather than genuine local market weakness.
insights.speaker: Ed Sperling
insights.so_what: The India flatness is likely a reporting artifact rather than a real demand signal, cautioning investors against reading the raw regional EDA figures for India as evidence of weak design activity growth there.
insights.locator: paragraph on South Korea and India

insights.claim: By category in Q1 2026: IC physical design and verification grew 8.3% to $751.3 million, PCB and MCM revenue increased 4.8% to $419.2 million, SIP (semiconductor IP) increased 14.1% to $2.333 billion, and services grew 6.5% to $226.4 million — with SIP now the largest single category by revenue.
insights.speaker: Ed Sperling
insights.so_what: SIP's outsized $2.333 billion scale and 14.1% growth confirm semiconductor IP licensing is now the largest EDA-adjacent revenue category, ahead of physical design/verification tools — relevant for evaluating IP-focused vendors like Arm, SiFive, and IP licensing businesses within larger EDA firms.

insights.claim: Industry employment across the EDA/IP/services sector increased 12.6% year over year in Q1 2026, reaching 72,544 people, tracking closely with the overall revenue growth rate.
insights.speaker: Ed Sperling
insights.so_what: Headcount growth roughly matching revenue growth (12.6% vs 12.7%) suggests the EDA industry is scaling organically rather than through productivity gains alone, a relevant labor-cost signal for margin modeling of EDA vendors.
